title: mentha arvensis subsp. parietariifolia
intro: Mentha arvensis subsp. parietariifolia, also known as Japanese wild mint, is a subspecies of the wild mint plant that is native to East Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Japanese wild mint include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and reduce stress and anxiety, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Japanese wild mint can be consumed as a tea, added to salads, or used in cooking, and it is also used in traditional herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and essential oils.
